summary: A polished, music-driven montage of heroic, sun-lit portraits of everyday American workers (a man in a camo cap at a union hall, a man in a red polo at a construction wall, a hard-hat-and-safety-vest worker against blue sky) with a recurring cinematic serif title card 'This is what we're FIGHTING FOR,' closing on the DOL seal and 'Lori Chavez-Deremer, Secretary of Labor.' The treatment is emotional and aspirational, framing the administration's 'American Workers First' agenda as a noble fight via slow hero shots and stylized typography. The empty transcript, music bed, color grading, and branded end card make it produced promotional spectacle rather than a raw event clip.

script.scene: Indoor union-hall shot: an older man in a camouflage ball cap and black polo looks up smiling, a yellow union banner ('...ARN / TO LIVE / TO S...') behind him; overlaid serif title 'This is what we're FIGHTING FOR.'
Outdoor shot of a bearded man in a red striped polo with sunglasses on his head, gesturing against a concrete wall and blue sky; same 'This is what we're FIGHTING FOR' title overlay.
Hero close-up of a blonde construction worker in a white hard hat, mirrored sunglasses, and orange safety vest against bright blue sky; same title card overlay.
Fade toward black with a partial circular Department of Labor seal/eagle crest emerging.
Black end card: white Department of Labor seal ('DEPARTMENT OF LABOR / UNITED STATES OF AMERICA') with 'LORI CHAVEZ-DEREMER / SECRETARY of LABOR.'

notable_text: This is what we're FIGHTING FOR
DEPARTMENT OF LABOR UNITED STATES OF AMERICA
We'll never give up our fight to put American Workers FIRST
